seconds_v2.desc: A green ceramic tumbler holds a freshly poured pale yellow-green candle, its wick held upright by a black-and-white wick-centering bar laid across the rim; it sits on a mottled white work surface with a clear glass pouring beaker behind it and a stainless wire rack at upper left.
Same setup, the candle left undisturbed to set; the smooth liquid wax surface inside the green vessel reflects the overhead light, wick clip steady across the top.
Static shot of the green tumbler candle curing on the bench; the empty glass pour pitcher and wire chafing-dish rack remain in the background, a folded rag at far left.
The poured green candle continues to rest, wick centered by the bar; nothing moves, the wax cooling in the ceramic cup.
Unchanged view of the green container candle setting on the white table, glass beaker and wire rack behind, the wax still glossy as it begins to cool.
The candle sits curing, wick held straight by the centering clip; faint surface sheen on the wax inside the green tumbler under the work light.
Same stationary scene of the freshly poured green candle left to set, pouring pitcher and rack in the background of the workspace.
The green ceramic candle continues cooling undisturbed, wick bar across the rim, surroundings unchanged.
Final second: the poured green tumbler candle rests on the bench setting, wick centered, glass beaker and wire rack behind it, the wax surface still smooth and glossy.
